description: Apache Log4j 2 JNDI lookup RCE — Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228). OOB detection ONLY: the payload carries the literal COLLABORATOR token which the runtime substitutes with a real Burp Collaborator subdomain; a collaborator interaction confirms the vulnerable log sink. Never hardcode a callback domain (Rule 9a).

log4shell_jndi_2021.description: CVE-2021-44228 — Log4j 2 evaluates ${jndi:ldap://...} lookups in logged strings, causing the server to resolve/fetch an attacker-controlled JNDI endpoint. Detection = inject the COLLABORATOR JNDI probe into commonly-logged inputs and confirm an out-of-band DNS/LDAP interaction.

probes.description: JNDI LDAP lookup probe. If a Collaborator DNS/LDAP interaction fires, the input reached a vulnerable Log4j sink (CVE-2021-44228). Runtime replaces COLLABORATOR with a live Collaborator subdomain.
JNDI DNS lookup variant — some deployments block LDAP egress but resolve DNS. Collaborator DNS interaction confirms the vulnerable Log4j lookup. COLLABORATOR substituted at runtime.
Nested-lookup variant — embeds ${hostName} so the OOB interaction also leaks the target hostname label, proving evaluation of nested Log4j lookups. COLLABORATOR substituted at runtime.
